title: cistus berthelotianus var. leucophyllus
intro: Cistus berthelotianus var. leucophyllus, also known as the white rockrose, is a flowering plant species in the rockrose family (Cistaceae) that is native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins in its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Cistus berthelotianus var. leucophyllus is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
